title: The Forest That Remembered
description: The enchanted forest is not corrupted but redesigned — paths that funnel and converge, movement channeled toward particular clearings, and a second Tempo calling card pinned to a tree.

lines: The enchanted forest was not corrupted. That would have been simpler.
It had been redesigned. Paths that should have diverged converged instead. Clearings appeared at intervals too regular for nature, positioned to funnel movement toward particular points.
Something had arranged this forest the way you arrange furniture in a room where you expect company.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: We took the left fork twice. We should be somewhere else entirely.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: My compass has been spinning since we entered.

steps.text: They found the note pinned to a tree they had not passed before — or thought they hadn't. A folded square of paper, sealed with the image of a running hound.
The note read: 'You've been here three times now. I've been watching. — T.'

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: That's not possible. We've only just entered.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Someone designed these paths to converge. Every route leads back here. That's not an accident — it's architecture.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: They wanted to see how we handled being disoriented. There's a difference between lost and observed.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: So we've been observed. Three times, apparently. I hope our second visit was better than our first.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: We don't remember our first visit. That's the point.

lines: The paths ahead looked identical to the ones they'd already taken.
The forest offered no help and no apology.
